title: Capability-based native imports with deny-by-default policy
purpose: Any loaded .beam module can currently call meridian_ffi:run_cmd/1 (arbitrary shell) and read_file/write_file (unrestricted filesystem) with no capability check — the module inherits the full ambient authority of the host process. For a runtime aimed at executing untrusted Gleam, this voids the safety property entirely. This brief introduces a capability taxonomy on every registered native, a CapabilityPolicy trait evaluated at import resolution, and a deny-by-default least-authority policy so that dangerous operations require explicit host grants.
task: Add a Capability enum to native entries, a CapabilityPolicy trait with a LeastAuthorityPolicy default, and wire the policy into the loader's import resolution. Denied imports are bound to a undef-trapping stub (not panic, not silent success). Tag all existing natives with their capability class. The host can supply a custom policy to grant broader capabilities when needed. Scope: beamr core only — beamr-meridian wiring is Scott's responsibility.

requirements.title: Capability enum
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a Capability enum with variants Pure, Clock, Entropy, and ExternalIo, carried by every NativeEntry.
acceptance: Capability enum in crates/beamr/src/native/capability.rs with #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
Pure: no side effects, no external I/O, no nondeterminism (arithmetic, type checks, list ops)
Clock: reads wall-clock time (erlang:system_time, os:timestamp, timer functions)
Entropy: consumes randomness (rand:uniform, crypto operations)
ExternalIo: shell commands, filesystem, network, environment variables
NativeEntry gains a capability: Capability field

requirements.title: Tag all existing natives with capability class
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL tag every registered BIF and NIF with its correct Capability class. THE SYSTEM SHALL default to Pure for arithmetic, comparison, type-test, list, tuple, map, binary, and guard BIFs. THE SYSTEM SHALL tag time-related BIFs as Clock, rand BIFs as Entropy, and meridian_ffi/os:* BIFs as ExternalIo.
acceptance: All register_* functions pass a Capability parameter or use a tagged registration helper
erlang:+/2, erlang:length/1, lists:sort/1, etc. are Pure
erlang:system_time/0, timer:send_after/3, os:timestamp/0 are Clock
rand:uniform/0, rand:uniform/1 are Entropy
meridian_ffi:run_cmd/1, meridian_ffi:read_file/1, meridian_ffi:write_file/2, os:getenv/1, os:putenv/2 are ExternalIo
No native is registered without an explicit capability tag — the API requires it

requirements.title: CapabilityPolicy trait and LeastAuthorityPolicy
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a CapabilityPolicy trait with a single method: fn is_granted(&self, capability: Capability) -> bool. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a LeastAuthorityPolicy that grants only Pure. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an AllCapabilitiesPolicy that grants everything (for trusted embedders).
acceptance: CapabilityPolicy trait in capability.rs with is_granted(&self, Capability) -> bool
LeastAuthorityPolicy: is_granted returns true only for Capability::Pure
AllCapabilitiesPolicy: is_granted returns true for all variants
CapabilitySet struct for building custom policies: from_slice(&[Capability]), grants(Capability) -> bool
All policies are Send + Sync for use across scheduler threads

requirements.title: Denial stub for denied imports
requirements.spec: WHEN a native import's capability is not granted by the active policy, THE SYSTEM SHALL bind the import to a denial stub that returns ExecError::Undef when called. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT panic, silently succeed, or leave the import unresolved.
acceptance: denial_stub function in capability.rs: fn(&[Term], &mut ProcessContext) -> Result<Term, Term> that returns Err(undef atom)
DenialMode enum with Undef variant (extensible for future Trap variant)
The stub preserves the correct arity so it occupies the import table slot correctly
A test calling a denied native receives a {undef, {Module, Function, Args}} error, not a panic

requirements.title: Policy evaluation at import resolution
requirements.spec: WHEN the loader resolves native imports, THE SYSTEM SHALL evaluate each resolved NativeEntry against the active CapabilityPolicy. IF the policy denies the capability, THE SYSTEM SHALL replace the resolved target with the denial stub. THE SYSTEM SHALL log denied imports in the unresolved-import report with reason 'capability denied'.
acceptance: resolve_imports in load.rs accepts a &dyn CapabilityPolicy parameter
For each resolved Native import, the entry's capability is checked against the policy
Denied entries are replaced with ResolvedImportTarget::Native pointing to the denial stub
The unresolved-import report includes denied imports with their capability class and denial reason
load_module's public API accepts an optional policy (defaults to LeastAuthorityPolicy)

requirements.title: Scheduler API accepts capability policy
requirements.spec: WHEN Scheduler is constructed, THE SYSTEM SHALL accept an optional CapabilityPolicy (defaulting to AllCapabilitiesPolicy for backwards compatibility with existing embedders). WHEN hot_load_module is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL use the scheduler's policy for import resolution.
acceptance: Scheduler::new or Scheduler::builder accepts an Arc<dyn CapabilityPolicy>
Default policy is AllCapabilitiesPolicy (backwards compatible — existing code works unchanged)
hot_load_module passes the policy to the loader's resolve_imports
Aion/beamr-meridian can continue using AllCapabilitiesPolicy until they opt into restrictions
A scheduler with LeastAuthorityPolicy denies meridian_ffi:run_cmd/1 at load time

requirements.title: Tests for capability-based import denial
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL include tests verifying that denied capabilities produce undef errors and granted capabilities work normally.
acceptance: Test: LeastAuthorityPolicy — Pure BIF (erlang:+/2) resolves normally, ExternalIo NIF (meridian_ffi:run_cmd/1) resolves to denial stub
Test: AllCapabilitiesPolicy — all natives resolve normally
Test: custom CapabilitySet granting [Pure, Clock] — Clock BIF resolves, Entropy BIF denied
Test: calling a denied stub returns undef error with correct MFA
Test: denied imports appear in unresolved-import report with capability-denied reason
Existing tests pass (they use AllCapabilitiesPolicy by default)

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement per-process or per-module capability policies — the policy is per-scheduler (VM-wide)
SHALL NOT implement runtime capability revocation — capabilities are checked once at import resolution
SHALL NOT remove or disable meridian_ffi NIFs — they are tagged ExternalIo and denied by policy, not deleted
SHALL NOT implement capability inheritance or delegation between processes
SHALL NOT change the default policy for existing embedders — AllCapabilitiesPolicy is the backwards-compatible default
verification: cargo test --workspace passes with all existing tests plus new capability tests
Every NativeEntry in the codebase carries an explicit Capability tag — grep for NativeEntry construction confirms no untagged entries
LeastAuthorityPolicy denies meridian_ffi:run_cmd/1 and os:getenv/1 at load time
Denied import produces undef error at call time, not panic
